text.title: Mocking Strategies Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every mocking decision MUST enforce. Hard rules — operator input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Mock at the architectural boundary (filesystem, network, time, randomness, third-party services) — NEVER inside the system under test.
rule.rationale: Internal mocks couple tests to implementation; boundary mocks let internal refactoring proceed without test churn.

rule.statement: Every Python mock MUST be created with spec=X or autospec=True. Bare MagicMock() is forbidden in production test code.
rule.rationale: Without spec, MagicMock returns a new Mock for any attribute access; typos and signature drift become silent passes.

rule.statement: Patch where the name is looked up (the importing module), NOT where it is defined.
rule.rationale: Python resolves names at call time in the importing namespace. Patching the origin leaves the local reference intact.

rule.statement: Do NOT mock third-party libraries directly. Wrap them in an adapter you own, then mock the adapter.
rule.rationale: Direct mocking of third-party APIs makes tests fragile to library updates. The adapter pattern gives you one place to absorb the API contract.

rule.statement: JavaScript suites MUST call vi.clearAllMocks() or jest.clearAllMocks() in afterEach. Python suites MUST use pytest-mock's mocker fixture (auto-cleanup), NOT bare @patch decorators.
rule.rationale: Mock state leaks between tests cause order-dependent flakes that don't reproduce locally.

rule.statement: Time-dependent code MUST be tested with freezegun (Python), @sinonjs/fake-timers (JS), or clockwork (Go). Never test against real datetime.now().
rule.rationale: Real-time tests are nondeterministic by definition; they pass at noon and fail at midnight.

rule.statement: For stateful dependencies used across many tests (repositories, caches), prefer a Fake implementation over recurring Mock expectations.
rule.rationale: Fakes encapsulate the contract once; Mocks re-encode it per test, multiplying maintenance.

rule.statement: When verify_calls_needed == false, the spec MUST classify the dependency as Stub (return canned values, no call assertions).
rule.rationale: Stubs document return-value expectations without coupling to call patterns.

rule.statement: When verify_calls_needed == true, the spec MUST classify the dependency as Mock with autospec, and pin the expected call signature.
rule.rationale: Mock-with-autospec catches signature drift and required-arg omissions at test time.

rule.statement: When dependency is stateful AND used in >3 test files, the spec MUST classify as Fake (working in-memory implementation).
rule.rationale: Beyond 3 reuse points, the per-test Mock cost exceeds the one-time Fake authoring cost.
